front.title: AI in Dystopian Futures
front.subtitle: Exploring the cautionary role of artificial intelligence in speculative literature and cinema.

back.text:
Dystopian science fiction often utilizes artificial intelligence as a narrative tool to explore themes of control, surveillance, and the loss of human agency. These stories frequently depict AI as an omnipresent governing force or a tool used by authoritarian regimes to enforce social order. By presenting extreme scenarios where technology supersedes human ethics, these works serve as philosophical thought experiments regarding the alignment of machine goals with human values.

Common tropes in this genre include the 'rogue superintelligence' and the 'automated bureaucracy,' where algorithmic decision-making becomes cold, rigid, and inescapable. While these depictions are often hyperbolic, they reflect real-world societal anxieties concerning data privacy, algorithmic bias, and the potential for technological overreach. In a museum context, these narratives highlight the importance of responsible AI development and the necessity of maintaining human oversight in automated systems.
